config_file_locations.windows_note: Unity stores PlayerPrefs in the Windows registry under HKCU\Software\<CompanyName>\<ProductName>. Graphics settings may also be stored as flat files in %APPDATA%\LocalLow\<CompanyName>\<ProductName>\ depending on how the game implements its settings. Always check for a game-specific profile before applying generic Unity keys.

keys.notes: Registry key names include a hash suffix that varies by game. Check HKCU\Software\<CompanyName>\<ProductName>\ to find the correct key name for a specific game.
Exclusive fullscreen (0) is lowest latency. Borderless (1) is convenient for multi-monitor but adds DWM composition overhead.

command_line_args.effect: Force windowed (0) or fullscreen (1) at launch.
Force Vulkan renderer instead of DirectX. May improve or reduce performance depending on GPU and driver version.
Force DirectX 11 renderer. Useful fallback if DX12 causes crashes.

notes: Unity does not have a universal config file. Each game implements its own settings persistence — some use PlayerPrefs (registry), some use JSON, some use custom formats. Always use a game-specific profile when available.
The registry key name hash suffix (e.g. _h182942802) is deterministic per key name but varies between games if the company/product name differs.
Unity games built with IL2CPP or Mono both share the same PlayerPrefs registry location pattern.
Unity 2019.3+ introduced the Input System package; games using the old Input Manager behave differently for controller detection. This does not affect graphics config.
